license.attribution: TabICL (soda-inria), BSD-3-Clause. Checkpoints: HF jingang/TabICL (tabicl-classifier-v2 / tabicl-regressor-v2).

_note: TabICL v2 exported at the released v2 architecture, weight-free (opset 18). H is DYNAMIC (no fixed feature bucket, no padding, no cat_mask, no d input). Input y carries ONLY the training labels: its length S IS train_size (there is no separate train_size / cat_mask / d input). Output logits[1,T,C]: rows >= S are the test predictions (rows < S carry the model's own values and are ignored). C = 10 for classification (max_classes). For REGRESSION C = 1: a single real-valued POINT ESTIMATE per row. The graph reduces the 999-quantile head with sklearn TabICLRegressor's default (output_type='mean' == mean over the monotonic quantiles; the mean is sort-invariant) and applies an in-graph StandardScaler + inverse (matching sklearn's y_scaler_). REGRESSION TARGET CONTRACT: the graph is SELF-CONTAINED — the engine feeds RAW train targets in y and reads logits[1,T,1] directly as the real-valued prediction; the engine must NOT z-score the target and must NOT inverse-transform the output (feature z-scoring via tabicl_v2_minimal is unchanged; TabICL tolerates it). ARCHITECTURE NOTE: the classifier and regressor checkpoints differ — tabicl-classifier-v2 has bias_free_ln=False (391 tensors), tabicl-regressor-v2 has bias_free_ln=True (347 tensors); each graph is exported to match its checkpoint, so real-weight injection covers 100%. tensor_map: injection is name-based (onnx 'm.<key>' <- safetensors '<key>', with an 'm.' fallback), so the shared classification map (../resources/tensor_map_tabicl_classification.json) drives both graphs; the regression map (../resources/tensor_map_tabicl_regression.json) is committed for convert_weights.py. Preprocessing: TabICL standardizes X internally on train rows; the sklearn wrapper additionally ordinal-encodes + imputes + shuffles features and ensembles 8 members — a single-member minimal z-score+ordinal C++ preprocess is close but not identical (see FEASIBILITY). Verified real-weight regression (single member, ORT + injected regressor weights): california_housing R2=0.82 (MSE 0.27) vs mean-predictor baseline MSE 1.52, tracking sklearn TabICLRegressor at corr 0.9999.
_weights_note: HF ships PyTorch .ckpt (pickle); the engine injects safetensors. Run once per task: `uv run python tools/export_tabicl/convert_weights.py <classification|regression> ~/.cache/anofox-tabfm`, then model := 'tabicl-v2'.
